headline: Microsoft (MSFT) Stock Trades Up, Here Is Why
summary: Shares of technology giant Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) jumped 3.3% in the afternoon session after Snowflake's impressive Q1 results provided clearer evidence that the "SaaSpocalypse", a rolling selloff that had erased approximately $2 trillion from software market values since late 2025 on fears that AI would make subscription software obsolete, had been overstated for platforms sitting at the centre of AI workflows.

headline: Dell Stock Rises 40% as AI Revenue Supercharges Growth
summary: Dell Technologies  revenue is growing at an accelerating pace as demand soars for hardware that powers artificial intelligence.  For the fiscal first-quarter, the PC and server maker reported adjusted earnings of $4.86 per share on revenue of $43.8 billion.  In the same period last year, Dell posted adjusted earnings of $1.55 a share on revenue of $23.4 billion.

headline: Buy Or Sell Microsoft Stock At $425?
summary: Microsoft (MSFT) stock has declined roughly 12% over the last six months and currently trades around $425, well off its October 2025 peak of $542. headline: Oracle (ORCL) Shares Skyrocket, What You Need To Know
summary: Shares of enterprise software giant Oracle (NYSE:ORCL) jumped 6.9% in the afternoon session after Snowflake's impressive earnings results provided the clearer evidence that the "SaaSpocalypse" — a rolling selloff that had erased approximately $2 trillion from software market values since late 2025 on fears that AI would make subscription software obsolete — had been overstated for platforms sitting at the centre of AI workflows.

headline: Nvidia Dropped a Cryptic Clue About Its Next Big Move Ahead of GTC Event
summary: Nvidia  on Friday posted a cryptic social media post that seemed to give credence to rumors that the company will make a chip for Windows PCs and announce it at the chipmaker’s GTC Taipei convention next week.  The post read “A new era of PC,” and then listed two numbers which appear to be the coordinates of the Taipei Music Center.  Huang’s presentation is slated to begin on Monday at 11 a.m. Taipei time, which is Sunday at 11 p.m. U.S. Eastern Time.

headline: Micron Isn’t Nvidia. It’s Time to Take Your Foot Off the Gas with MU Stock.
summary: Investors pricing in a decade of dominance for Micron are falling into a dangerous trap by ignoring the physical realities of the semiconductor market. The company is selling standardized hardware, and once competitors scale their own production using the exact same ASML technology, pricing power will collapse.

content.title: Micron Isn’t Nvidia. It’s Time to Take Your Foot Off the Gas with MU Stock.
content.summary: Investors pricing in a decade of dominance for Micron are falling into a dangerous trap by ignoring the physical realities of the semiconductor market. The company is selling standardized hardware, and once competitors scale their own production using the exact same ASML technology, pricing power will collapse.
